status_definitions.tested: The named implementation evidence passed the focused control asserted by this row.
status_definitions.implemented: Production behavior exists and named focused evidence is present, but a broader backend, integration, or release gate remains.
status_definitions.pending_validation: Implementation is present, but the required backend or integration validation is still running or blocked from final evidence.
status_definitions.partial: Relevant production or test foundations exist, but the complete target invariant is not proven.
status_definitions.planned: The target control or its required evidence is not complete. This is not a passing result.

tests.threat: Caller forges forwarded identity, role, tenant, or client address headers.
tests.control: Exact expected Host, trusted-proxy canonicalization, forwarding-header stripping and server-side identity/tenant binding.
tests.expected: Non-loopback listeners require an exact expected Host; untrusted forwarding headers reject and trusted forwarding metadata is stripped before application dispatch.
tests.evidence_required: Gateway tests through trusted and untrusted proxy hops with duplicate and conflicting headers.

tests.threat: Authenticated principal crosses tenant boundaries or exploits missing database isolation.
tests.control: Single tenant binding plus PostgreSQL RLS and transaction-local tenant context.
tests.expected: Cross-tenant reads and writes fail through every API and direct repository operation.
tests.evidence_required: Multi-tenant API and PostgreSQL RLS suite using synthetic tenants.
evidence: live PostgreSQL/RLS cross-tenant and four-kind governance lifecycle proof

tests.threat: Session fixation, replay, stale privilege, or incomplete multi-replica revocation.
tests.control: Opaque bound sessions, rotation, expiry, credential/policy epochs, and durable revocation.
tests.expected: Fixed, expired, revoked, replayed, or privilege-stale sessions are rejected on every replica.
tests.evidence_required: Clock-controlled fixation/expiry/revocation tests across at least two gateway replicas.
evidence: deployed two-gateway chaos acceptance is tracked separately from source verification

tests.threat: Unicode detector offsets are applied as bytes incorrectly, masking the wrong content.
tests.control: Per-field Unicode-scalar to UTF-8 byte-range conversion with bounded typed findings.
tests.expected: Finding locations and masking remain correct for multibyte prefixes and nested tool arguments.
tests.evidence_required: Focused Presidio adapter Unicode and nested-argument regression tests.

tests.threat: Adversarial JSON causes unbounded inspection work or false full coverage.
tests.control: Depth, value, byte, finding, detector, reason and tag limits with explicit coverage.
tests.expected: Work remains bounded and truncated/opaque inputs never report full coverage.
tests.evidence_required: Limit boundary tests plus retained parser/walker fuzz corpus.

tests.threat: Unsupported modality or unavailable detector silently bypasses sensitive-data controls.
tests.control: Explicit partial/unsupported coverage with mode- and policy-specific enforcement.
tests.expected: Observe records would-deny; enforce constrains/denies; bank denies required unresolved inspection.
tests.evidence_required: Text, image, audio, file, non-UTF8, timeout and detector-outage matrix.

tests.threat: Sensitive output spans stream chunks and escapes a per-chunk detector.
tests.control: Incremental bounded literal inspection with overlap, plus bounded full precommit inspection for bank text/SSE.
tests.expected: Boundary-spanning literals are detected under both transport-equivalent chunk models; bank text/SSE requiring full inspection is buffered and masked before release; direct unsupported transports are not reported as Full.
tests.evidence_required: Deterministic randomized shared-inspector corpus plus bounded full-stream masking and coverage-selection regressions.

tests.threat: Policy result changes with rule order, hash-map iteration, or missing attributes.
tests.control: Pure deterministic typed PDP with stable precedence and reason codes.
tests.expected: Equivalent inputs produce identical effect, obligations and reason codes; mandatory unknowns fail safely.
tests.evidence_required: Golden vectors and randomized rule/input permutation properties.

tests.threat: Conflicting obligations weaken a deny, region, retention, tool, audit, or provider constraint.
tests.control: Most-restrictive effect precedence and typed obligation conflict resolution.
tests.expected: Deny is absorbing and unresolved mandatory conflicts deny rather than choose a weaker value.
tests.evidence_required: Pairwise and generated obligation merge properties.

tests.threat: Invalid, stale or revoked policy snapshot is served from cache or LKG.
tests.control: Checksum validation, atomic activation, revisioned cache keys and invalidation precedence.
tests.expected: Invalid snapshots never activate; revocation beats cache/LKG; bank denies without valid mandatory state.
tests.evidence_required: Corruption, refresh race, activation acknowledgement, invalidation and LKG outage suite.

tests.threat: Maker self-approves, replays, races, or approves a changed policy revision.
tests.control: Independent quorum bound to immutable checksum with transactional activation.
tests.expected: Self, stale, replayed, expired and conflicting approvals fail; one valid activation is audited.
tests.evidence_required: Model/state-machine and real-database concurrency tests.

tests.threat: Execution approval is replayed for a changed request, tenant, policy, provider or operation.
tests.control: Decision/input/revision-bound approval with atomic expiry/use/idempotency consumption.
tests.expected: Mismatched, revoked, expired or exhausted approval denies and cannot expand policy eligibility.
tests.evidence_required: Mutation/replay/race matrix matching sample 05.

tests.threat: Break-glass becomes a broad persistent bypass or exceeds its retention scope.
tests.control: Retention-purge-only independent approval with a one-hour TTL ceiling, explicit revocation, bounded purge batches and mandatory audit.
tests.expected: Non-active, expired, revoked or out-of-scope approvals deny; tenant, credential-scope, retention and audit invariants remain enforced.
tests.evidence_required: Approval lifecycle, exact scope/expiry authorization, durable storage and mandatory-audit tests.

tests.threat: A high soft score restores a provider excluded by classification, region, retention or approval.
tests.control: Hard compliance filter before deterministic eligible-set-only scoring.
tests.expected: Ineligible providers are never scored or selected under any soft input.
tests.evidence_required: Generated provider/constraint sets and seven synthetic scenario assertions.

tests.threat: Floating-point, iteration or replica variance makes routing unauditable.
tests.control: Bounded integer/fixed-point components and stable tie-break ordering.
tests.expected: The same eligible snapshot/input produces byte-stable score components and selection across replicas.
tests.evidence_required: Cross-process golden vectors and permutation properties.

tests.threat: Load, transport backoff or health heuristics break a hard continuation binding.
tests.control: Hard affinity precedes fresh heuristics; explicit revocation alone overrides eligibility.
tests.expected: Existing owner remains selected under local heuristics; tenant mismatch/revocation fails safely.
tests.evidence_required: Existing runtime affinity regressions extended with tenant and governance epochs.

tests.threat: Proxy retries or rotates after model output has begun.
tests.control: One response commitment boundary and no postcommit retry/rotation.
tests.expected: Postcommit failure is surfaced naturally; no second upstream target receives the continuation.
tests.evidence_required: HTTP/SSE/WebSocket fault injection before and after first committed chunk.

tests.threat: Governance mutation succeeds while its mandatory audit or SIEM outbox record is lost.
tests.control: One database transaction for business state, audit chain head/event and outbox.
tests.expected: Failure at every statement/commit boundary rolls back all state and returns no success.
tests.evidence_required: SQLite and PostgreSQL transactional fault matrix.
evidence: atomic session revoke plus audit/outbox SQLite and live PostgreSQL coverage

tests.threat: Audit events are deleted, reordered, altered, forked or contain raw sensitive data.
tests.control: Versioned content-free schema, per-partition hash chain and deterministic verification.
tests.expected: Tamper/fork is detected at the first affected event and synthetic content canaries never appear.
tests.evidence_required: Existing audit-log tests extended to governance schemas, concurrent heads and canary scan.
evidence: bounded tenant audit export storage and HTTP assertions

tests.threat: SIEM retries duplicate, lose, poison or indefinitely lease events.
tests.control: Transactional outbox, idempotent event IDs, bounded leases/retries and visible dead-letter state.
tests.expected: Every committed event is retained and eventually delivered or visibly dead-lettered without hot-path SIEM calls.
tests.evidence_required: Sink outage, duplicate ack, worker crash, lease expiry, poison and backlog-capacity suite.
evidence: PostgreSQL claim-token lease and separate finalize transactions passed live
SQLite exporter outage, retry and dead-letter tests

tests.threat: Secret values enter config, policy, logs, audit, errors or diagnostics.
tests.control: Opaque secret references, redacted types and repository/runtime canary scans.
tests.expected: Raw canary values are rejected at boundaries and absent from every captured artifact.
tests.evidence_required: Existing redaction/secret-store tests plus end-to-end canary scan.

tests.threat: A projected secret escapes its configured provider namespace, survives a requested-version mismatch, or is read through an unsafe filesystem path.
tests.control: Purpose-owned configuration selects the provider namespace; the bounded projected-secret adapter enforces provider name, exact version, anchored private files and atomic projection generations.
tests.expected: Provider mismatch, traversal, symlink escape, unsafe permissions and stale versions deny; atomic injector rotation is observed without restart.
tests.evidence_required: Projected-secret namespace, version rotation and filesystem-boundary tests.

tests.threat: Migration or rollback mixes incompatible schemas or loses tenant/audit/policy state.
tests.control: Versioned migrations plus authenticated AES-256-GCM backup, isolated restore and governance-link verification.
tests.expected: Old/new supported images interoperate and restored durable state preserves isolation, hashes and pointers.
tests.evidence_required: SQLite/PostgreSQL forward/back compatibility, export/import and point-in-time restore drill.
evidence: npm run ci:backup-restore-drill: final synthetic RPO 2.076 s, RTO 1.506 s, governance fingerprint and audit-link verification

tests.threat: Replica race or Redis loss creates split-brain activation, approval use or session state.
tests.control: PostgreSQL durable authority, optimistic concurrency/leases and rebuildable Redis coordination.
tests.expected: One authoritative mutation wins; Redis loss degrades boundedly and rebuilds without losing durable state.
tests.evidence_required: At least two replicas with concurrent activation/use, Redis restart and DB failover.
evidence: two live PostgreSQL repositories enforce atomic reservation/accounting
Redis-backed boundary proof passed; Redis restart and database failover remain deployment tests

tests.threat: HTTP, SSE, WebSocket, compatibility or Gemini paths bypass governed inspection/policy/audit.
tests.control: One application admission/decision boundary plus architecture dependency guards.
tests.expected: Every supported channel records the same required stage/revision evidence before provider dispatch.
tests.evidence_required: Channel parity tests and CI guard forbidding direct provider dispatch from adapters.

tests.threat: Local proxy converts upstream errors into misleading quota/governance errors.
tests.control: Pass-through after upstream response; local stable errors only for pre-upstream local failure.
tests.expected: Generic upstream 429/status/body/stream is preserved; local precommit exhaustion is a local service-unavailable result.
tests.evidence_required: Existing runtime quota/transport transparency regressions plus governed precommit failures.

tests.threat: Caller-controlled tenant, key, epoch, route or content values create unbounded metrics cardinality or leak identifiers.
tests.control: Aggregate gateway counters with fixed metric names and no caller-controlled labels.
tests.expected: Metrics aggregate usage without key, tenant, epoch or content labels.
tests.evidence_required: Focused metric rendering test plus production exporter/alert acceptance.
evidence: live alert, pager and achieved-SLO evidence remains deployment acceptance work

tests.threat: Bank label starts with a public listener, bypass, missing mandatory dependency, raw secret or broad egress.
tests.control: Typed bank startup validator and deployment security policy.
tests.expected: Every insecure combination rejects startup with a stable content-free reason.
tests.evidence_required: Generated configuration matrix plus Compose/Kubernetes/container policy tests.

tests.threat: Bank runtime fails open when identity, inspection, policy, registry, audit or secret dependencies fail.
tests.control: Mandatory fail-closed runtime matrix with no generic break-glass override.
tests.expected: Each missing mandatory dependency denies before upstream commit and emits bounded durable evidence when possible.
tests.evidence_required: Complete sample 07 scenario under dependency fault injection.
evidence: complete end-to-end dependency fault matrix pending

tests.threat: Container privilege, writable root, public bind or unrestricted egress bypasses application controls.
tests.control: Non-root/read-only runtime, private exposure, least privilege and allow-listed provider/secret/SIEM egress.
tests.expected: CI rejects insecure manifests and a deployed pod cannot reach unapproved endpoints.
tests.evidence_required: Manifest policy, image/provenance/vulnerability scan and network-policy connectivity tests.
evidence: deployed connectivity/image scan release evidence pending

tests.threat: Governance creates unbounded latency, allocation, cardinality or concurrency pressure.
tests.control: Immutable local snapshots, bounded stages/admission/labels and declared p99 budgets.
tests.expected: Disabled regression is at most 5%; governed local overhead at most 5 ms p99; PDP at most 1 ms; routing at most 2 ms.
tests.evidence_required: Five comparable samples with machine metadata, raw output, load/soak errors and variance.
evidence: 32/32 local load smoke at 67.38 ms p95 TTFT; 458-test primary runtime stress tranche passed
session-bank, audit/outbox pressure and external multi-replica soak remain deployment evidence
